# Std.Arg

<!-- generated by `flx docs build`; do not edit -->

*Generated from the `doc` declarations in `Arg.flx` by `flx docs build`. Examples are executed by `flx docs check`.*

Small helpers over program arguments.

Program arguments are the user arguments only, with argv[0] deliberately
excluded. Everything here observes process state and therefore uses
`{ Process }`.

*since 0.0.1 · status: implemented*

See also: `Std.Env`

## all

```flx
fn all() -> List<String> uses { Process }
```

All user arguments as a `List<String>`.

*since 0.0.1 · status: implemented*

## count

```flx
fn count() -> I64 uses { Process }
```

The number of user arguments.

*since 0.0.1 · status: implemented*

## at

```flx
fn at(i: I64) -> Option<String> uses { Process }
```

The argument at index i, or None when out of range.

*since 0.0.1 · status: implemented*

## has_flag

```flx
fn has_flag(name: String) -> Bool uses { Process }
```

True when an argument exactly equals the flag name.

**Sketch: check a flag** — illustration only, not checked:

```flx
if has_flag("--verbose") {
  // enable extra output
}
```

*since 0.0.1 · status: implemented*

See also: `value_after`

## value_after

```flx
fn value_after(name: String) -> Option<String> uses { Process }
```

The argument immediately after a flag, or None.

**Sketch: read an option value** — illustration only, not checked:

```flx
match value_after("--name") {
  Some(name) => { name }
  None => { "anonymous" }
}
```

*since 0.0.1 · status: implemented*

See also: `has_flag`
